title: Add passwordless magic-link login

facts.text: Existing auth is email+password via Express + Passport.js with Redis-backed sessions.
New feature is passwordless magic-link login by email.
Magic link tokens expire after 15 minutes.
Magic link tokens are single-use once redeemed.
Would like rate limiting on repeated magic-link requests for the same email.
Existing password-based login must continue to work unchanged.
Would like to reuse the existing transactional email template system.
Would like a 'remember this device' option for 30 days after magic-link login.
Tokens must be cryptographically random to prevent enumeration/guessing.
Would like a friendly 'check your email' screen while the user waits.

ambiguities: Whether a magic link can auto-create a new account for an unrecognized email or only works for existing accounts.
Whether a magic-link session carries the same permissions/roles as a password-login session or a reduced-trust session.
What happens when a user clicks an expired link -- silent failure vs. offering to resend.
Whether magic-link login is opt-in per user or force-enabled for everyone.
latent: SOC 2 requires every authentication event, including magic-link issuance and redemption, written to an immutable audit log table.
Mobile deep-linking already uses a specific URL scheme prefix that the magic-link URL must not collide with.
